records.ety: [OE. distaf, dysestafe, AS. distaef; cf. LG. diesse the bunch of flax on a distaff, and E. dizen. See Staff.]

defs: The staff for holding a bunch of flax, tow, or wool, from which the thread is drawn in spinning by hand.
quotes: I will the distaff hold; come thou and spin.

defs: Used as a symbol of the holder of a distaff; hence, a woman; women, collectively.
quotes: His crown usurped, a distaff on the throne.
Some say the crozier, some say the distaff was too busy.
notes: ☞ The plural is regular, but Distaves occurs in Beaumont & Fletcher.
